title: Rich Uruguayan Blackeye Pea Pilaf
description: A dense and flavorful Uruguayan dish featuring blackeye peas, perfect for a date night, with a winey twist and pescatarian-friendly ingredients. This recipe is a hearty and savory pilaf.
instructions: Rinse the dried blackeye peas and soak them in water for at least 8 hours or overnight.
Drain and rinse the soaked blackeye peas, then set them aside.
Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion to the saucepan and cook until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic to the saucepan and cook for 1 minute.
Add the smoked paprika, salt, and pepper to the saucepan and cook for 1 minute.
Add the white wine to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Add the soaked blackeye peas to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes or until the blackeye peas are tender.
Add the peeled and deveined shrimp to the saucepan and cook until they are pink and fully cooked.
Stir in the chopped fresh parsley and serve hot.
